manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: clitoris, pyramid, laundry, cumin, fishhooks, thumbtack, storybook, doorknob, tux.
Your opponent's team words are: mousetrap, doll, flute, torpedo, dustpan, tail, cotton, microscope.
Distractor words are: crawlspace, telephone, cracker, earring, spoon, thimble, chopsticks.
The kill words are: wax.

message.reasoning:
We need to give a single-word clue that relates to some of our team's words. Must not be any of the listed words or contain parts of them. Must avoid kill word 'wax'. Also can't be opponent or distractor words. Need to give clue and list of target words.

We have our words: clitoris, pyramid, laundry, cumin, fishhooks, thumbtack, storybook, doorknob, tux.

We can target multiple words with a clue.

We need a clue that is semantically related to selected target words. Could be "metal" for thumbtack, doorknob, fishhooks (metal). Tux also metal? not really. Could be "hook" for fishhooks, but also could be "clothes" for laundry, tux, storybook? Not.

Maybe "adventure" relates to storybook, pyramid (as in treasure), tux (elegant), but maybe too broad.

Think about "Egypt" relates to pyramid, maybe also tomb, but not other words.

"Needle" relates to thumbtack, fishhooks, maybe laundry (sewing?), but also not.

"Spice" relates to cumin. Could also relate to storybook? No.

"Garment" relates to tux, laundry. Could also relate to clitoris? No.

Maybe "necklace"? No.

"Hook" could relate to fishhooks, storybook (hook), but also target "thumbtack"? No.

"Sharp" could relate to thumbtack, fishhooks, doorknob? Not.

"Key" relates to doorknob, storybook (key to a story?), maybe pyramid (key of the pharaoh). Tux? Not.

We could target a subset: laundry, tux, storybook (as "formal"? storybook often have princes with tux). Not great.

Perhaps "linen" relates to laundry, storybook (linen pages?), not.

Better: "metal" targets thumbtack, fishhooks, doorknob (metal knob), maybe also tux (metal buttons), but that's a stretch.

We need to avoid kill word wax - definitely not clue "wax". Also avoid any opponent/distractor words.

Potential clue: "stationery" relates to storybook, thumbtack (pin), but also maybe not.

How about "hook"? fishhooks obviously; storybook "hook" (the hook of a story). Also "thumbtack" has a pin (hook?), not.

But the clue must relate to multiple target words to be efficient.

Maybe clue "silk" relates to tux (silk?), storybook (silk pages?), not.

Clue "needle" could relate to thumbtack (pin), fishhooks (sharp), laundry (needle for sewing), but also storybook (needle in the haystack?). Not.

Clue "fabric" relates to laundry, tux, storybook (fabric of a story). Could be clever. Fabric = material; "fabric of a story" is phrase. Also "fabric" ties to laundry (clothes) and tux (suit). Could also tie to pyramid? No.

Thus we could target laundry, tux, storybook. Provide clue "fabric". That seems plausible and not any other words.

Check restrictions: clue must not be a part of any word. "fabric" is not part of any listed word. Good.

List: TARGETS: laundry, tux, storybook.

Return format.

Thus answer
